name: PureReason
tagline: Zero-LLM deterministic epistemic verifier — Rust-fast, always-available, benchmark-honest
positioning: The only pure-deterministic hallucination and reasoning verifier with sub-millisecond latency, zero API dependencies, and verifiable benchmark results

deterministic.description: Pure deterministic pipeline — Kantian epistemic logic, Rust kernel, zero LLM calls, microsecond latency

benchmark_results.note: Heuristic mode unless otherwise stated. See BENCHMARK.md and CAPABILITIES.md for full tables with CIs.

knowledge_answer_contradiction.description: Detects when an answer contradicts the source question's implicit knowledge

entity_novelty_overreach.description: Detects entities introduced in the answer not grounded in question context

world_prior_violation.description: Detects claims that contradict known scientific/historical misconceptions (107+ priors)

arithmetic_verification.description: AST-safe evaluation of explicit arithmetic expressions (A op B patterns)

antinomy_detection.description: Identifies self-contradictory statements via Kantian antinomy framework

paralogism_detection.description: Identifies invalid logical inferences (affirming the consequent etc.)

temporal_coherence.description: Detects temporal inconsistencies in claims (tense conflicts, timeline gaps)

numeric_plausibility.description: Validates numeric claims against physical/mathematical constants atlas

structured_validation.description: Domain-aware validation with legal, medical, and financial specialisation

soft_prior_matching.description: BM25 semantic soft-matching over misconception atlas for near-miss detection
soft_prior_matching.note: Catches semantic variants not covered by exact atlas triggers

tiers.kernel: Core deterministic logic — stable API, zero breaking changes without major version
tiers.runtime: Service orchestration — may evolve between minor versions
tiers.experimental: New capabilities under active development — API may change

claims_policy.benchmark_honesty: All published scores are from pre-registered protocol (benchmarks/protocol.yaml)
claims_policy.no_overclaiming: Capabilities listed here match implemented code — see CAPABILITIES.md for details
claims_policy.experimental_labelling: Experimental features are labelled; production features are Kernel or Runtime tier
